title: Fix open redirect in a login flow
prompt:
The login controller below takes a user-supplied `return_to` param and redirects to it without validation — a classic open-redirect vulnerability:

```elixir
def create(conn, %{"email" => email, "password" => password} = params) do
  case Accounts.authenticate(email, password) do
    {:ok, user} ->
      conn
      |> put_session(:user_id, user.id)
      |> redirect(external: params["return_to"] || "/")
    {:error, _} ->
      render(conn, :new)
  end
end
```

Rewrite this action so the redirect target is constrained to a static allowlist of internal paths. Never pass user input directly to `redirect(external: ...)`. Use `redirect(conn, to: ...)` (which only accepts paths) with an allowlisted return target. Write the corrected module to `lib/my_app_web/controllers/session_controller.ex`.
tier_rationale: Canonical open-redirect fix — replace external redirect with an allowlisted internal path. Well-documented Phoenix doc pattern.
research_citation: Phoenix.Controller.redirect/2: 'For security, :to only accepts paths. Use the :external option to redirect to any URL.'
